items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 2, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed higher on Thursday, supported by gains in the telecommunications sector and a rebound in heavyweight banking stocks. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin traded higher at RM246,617. Following the trend, Ethereum traded higher at RM6,607, while Solana stood at RM319.

items.title: Malaysia reaffirms counter-terrorism commitment at UN High-Level Conference
items.summary: The ministry said that the Malaysian delegation, led by deputy secretary-general (Security) Datuk Abdul Halim Abdul Rahman, also delivered the country's official intervention statement during Session III: The Global Terrorism Threat Landscape. "During the session, Abdul Halim firmly presented Malaysia's strategic views on the dynamics of today's security threats. It said Abdul Halim also delivered Malaysia's National Statement at the UN General Assembly Hall in New York yesterday under Agenda Item 118: Report of the UN Secretary-General on the Ninth Global Counter-Terrorism Strategy.

items.title: Media Prima hosts TNB delegation to strengthen ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Media Prima Bhd today hosted a delegation from Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) as part of a corporate visit aimed at strengthening the longstanding relationship between the two organisations. The TNB delegation was led by its chief executive officer, Datuk Shamsul Ahmad, while Media Prima was represented by its group managing director, Datuk Rafiq Razali; chief executive officer of Media Prima Content & TV Networks and Primeworks Studios, Nini Yusof; and group managing editor of Media Prima News and Current Affairs Division, Jasbant Singh. Shamsul described the visit as an eye-opening experience and praised Media Prima's efforts in embracing technological innovation.

items.title: Grants, not loans: Ministry push to convert RM100mil TVET fund to ease trainee burden
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry is seeking Cabinet approval to convert a RM100 million allocation for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) financing under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) from loans into grants to ease the financial burden on trainees. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the allocation, announced under the government's TVET 2.0 agenda, was originally intended as a loan facility for eligible trainees, but the ministry hopes it can instead be channelled as grants. Ramanan said the proposed grants would ease the financial burden on trainees, many of whom have to leave their jobs temporarily to pursue skills training.

items.title: Iran warns US, Israel against attacks ahead of funeral processions for Khamenei
items.summary: DUBAI: An Iranian military commander warned the United States and Israel on Thursday against any attack on Iran as it prepares for the state funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in airstrikes on the first day of the war. On Wednesday, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi gave a similar warning that Teheran would deliver an immediate and powerful response to any threat against its people or leadership after comments by Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz that Iran's current Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was "marked for death". Iranian media reported heightened security measures during the funeral period, while the head of Iran's Civil Aviation Organisation said on Wednesday that temporary airspace restrictions would be implemented over several cities including Teheran and Mashhad.

items.title: Taiping elephants gaining weight, remain healthy at Tennoji Zoo
items.summary: TAIPING: The Taiping Municipal Council has assured the public that its three elephants housed at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan, are in good health and adapting well to their new environment. Council president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said the latest veterinary reports and daily monitoring by the Japanese zoo showed that Kelat, Dara and Amoi were active, healthy and receiving proper care. Akmal said all three elephants had also recorded weight gains since the previous update, reflecting progress from continuous health monitoring, dietary management and ongoing care.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat defers Competition Bill committee stage to Monday
items.summary: Winding up the debate, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the amendments were aimed at strengthening the enforcement capacity of the Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) in tackling anti-competitive practices, including cartels and abuse of dominant positions. He said government-linked companies (GLCs) might be classified as government entities if they acted on delegated authority or carried out functions on behalf of the federal, state or local governments. Yes, if the GLC acts on delegated authority from the government or carries out functions on behalf of the federal government, state government or local authority, then it may fall within the definition of a government entity.

items.title: Paris Baguette Malaysia goes fully under Singapore HQ
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Paris Baguette Malaysia has come under the full ownership of Paris Baguette's Southeast Asia regional headquarters in Singapore following Berjaya Food Bhd's exit. The company said the restructuring aligns Malaysia more closely with Paris Baguette's broader Southeast Asia growth strategy and reflects its long-term commitment to the local market. In addition, Paris Baguette also identified halal certification as one of its top strategic priorities, following recent halal certifications obtained in Singapore and Indonesia.

items.title: Cathay Pacific to resume Middle East flights
items.summary: HONG KONG: Hong Kong's flagship airline Cathay Pacific announced Thursday that it will resume flights to the Middle East, as the United States and Iran signal that efforts to end the war remain viable. Cathay suspended flights to Dubai and Riyadh at the end of February after the war broke out. It said Thursday it would resume daily passenger flights to Dubai and four-times-weekly passenger flights to Riyadh from Sept 1.

items.title: HR Ministry launches five-year plan to take Malaysian TVET global
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry today unveiled the Department of Skills Development Internationalisation Action Plan 2026-2030 and the SkillsMalaysia Global initiative to raise the standards and international recognition of Malaysia's Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) system. Launching the roadmap, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the initiatives would align the National Occupational Skills Standards with international benchmarks while strengthening global recognition of the Malaysian Skills Certificate. Describing the initiatives as a "game changer", he said they would help Malaysia overcome future economic challenges.

items.title: Belgium eyes tech transfer over rare earths from Malaysia, seeks deeper mineral cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Belgium seeks to strengthen rare earth mineral ties with Malaysia in its bid to reduce reliance on China, the kingdom’s deputy prime minister Maxime Prévot said today. However, Prévot stressed that Belgium wants to strengthen technology transfer with Malaysia in the critical mineral sector rather than merely increasing exports of resources. He said Malaysia could benefit from the expertise of Belgian companies like Solvay and Umicore to efficiently extract different elements within the rare earth supply chain.

items.title: Johor election: KJ urges Bersatu to follow Pas in backing BN
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin has called on Bersatu members and supporters to follow Pas's example and back Barisan Nasional candidates in seats where Perikatan Nasional is not contesting in the Johor polls. "I am also encouraging my friends in Bersatu to follow Pas's approach of supporting BN candidates in seats they are not contesting. If there is no PN candidate in a seat, we are also encouraging Bersatu voters to support BN.

items.title: Zahid: Malaysia's unemployment rate drops to 2.9 per cent
items.summary: Zahid said the matter was informed by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during yesterday's cabinet meeting. Also present were Kemas director-general Datuk Mohd Hanafiah Man, BN candidate for Bekok Tan Chong and BN candidate Tenang Mohd Azahar Ibrahim. Zahid, who is also rural and regional development minister, said the employability rate among TVET graduates under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) institutions was also excellent, at 99.5 per cent.

items.title: Households in Malaysia waste up to 97kg of food per person each year, stats show
items.summary: He said that based on the National Household Indicators Survey (NHIS) 2025, food waste is more significant for processed or cooked food compared to raw food, with 94.1 per cent of households reported to have thrown away processed or cooked food compared to 88.7 per cent for raw food. He said that 79.3 per cent of households dispose of food together with other household waste, while only 20.7 per cent practise food waste separation. As for processed or cooked food, he said rice (16.7 per cent) recorded the highest wastage rate, followed by vegetables (15.8 per cent) and food bought from outside (13.8 per cent).

items.title: Lawyer Ang denies defaming Loke, vows to fight suit
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Lawyer Ang Woei Shang has denied allegations by DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke that he defamed him, vowing to contest the lawsuit and considering a counterclaim. The suit filed by Loke arose from three videos published on Ang's Facebook page between Feb 26 and May 8, touching on issues including the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC), pork supply, the alleged "corporate mafia" controversy, DAP's relationship with Umno and the Selangor pig farming issue. Loke filed the suit at the High Court here last week.

items.title: Indonesia's Aceh flogs couple for kissing in TikTok video
items.summary: BANDA ACEH: Officials in Indonesia's staunchly Islamic Aceh province publicly flogged an unmarried couple on Thursday, an AFP reporter witnessed, after the pair kissed in a video posted to social media. Amnesty International Indonesia spokesman Haeril Halim said the punishment for the couple was "a horrifying act of discrimination." Another couple also received 27 lashes on Thursday for physical intimacy, while two men were caned 29 times and eight times, respectively, for gambling.

items.title: Malaysia to move away from collateral-based financing, says Liew
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia needs to develop alternative financing models that rely less on physical collateral and better support technology-based businesses, says Deputy Finance Minister Datuk Seri Liew Chin Tong. He said discussions were ongoing with Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan (SJPP), Credit Guarantee Corporation (CGC) and Bank Negara Malaysia on new financing approaches, although the matter remained at an early stage. Liew said industry proposals to strengthen non-traditional credit assessment would be welcomed.

items.title: Onn Hafiz: Beware of candidates promising 'moon and stars'
items.summary: Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also state BN chairman, urged the people to learn from past experiences where they were offered various pledges that remain unfulfilled to this day. He cited the experience of Simpang Renggam voters, claiming the constituency's former member of parliament made numerous promises but failed to deliver them, before eventually moving to contest in another state seat in these Johor polls. They were promised the moon and stars.

items.title: Selangor contributes 26.5pct to Malaysia's GDP, widening lead over KL and Johor
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Selangor recorded strong economic growth in 2025 with its gross domestic product (GDP) expanding by RM28 billion from the preceding year to hit RM460.1 billion. According to DOSM, Selangor was the main contributor to the national economy, followed by Kuala Lumpur whose GDP grew by RM13.2 billion to RM265.1 billion. Amirudin also said Selangor's economic growth of 6.3 per cent in 2025 outperformed Malaysia's overall economic growth rate of 5.2 per cent.

items.title: What does it mean to be excommunicated from the Catholic Church?
items.summary: THE Vatican said on Thursday that priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval were in schism with the wider Church and now excommunicated. What is excommunication? The Church considers excommunication as a "medicinal" penalty.

items.title: Job seeker says identity used in RM2.4mil scam syndicate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 26-year-old man who believed he had secured a customer service job claims he was duped into opening bank accounts later used by a scam syndicate to move RM2.4 million. The victim, known only as Jay, said he was instructed to open a bank account during a week-long training programme, purportedly for salary payments. Jay said he was later told his job application had been unsuccessful before being taken to several other banks to open more accounts.

items.title: Lim, Pua respond to Sultan: LRT3 costs RM9.72bil less
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Shah Alam LRT3 project was ultimately completed at a cost of RM21.93 billion — RM9.72 billion lower than the RM31.65 billion originally approved during the previous Barisan Nasional administration. They said the lower project cost would also reduce the government's debt burden, resulting in anticipated interest savings of up to RM10 billion over 25 to 30 years. Following the review, the Cabinet approved reducing the project cost from RM31.65 billion to RM16.63 billion, representing savings of RM15.02 billion.

items.title: MyLesen B2 assistance not a ticket for illegal street racing, warns Anthony Loke
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Youth and students who benefit from the MyLesen B2 programme must not view the assistance as a ticket to participate in illegal street racing. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative under the Madani government aims to provide low-income families and youth with the opportunity to obtain valid motorcycle licences safely and affordably. Also present were Transport Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Jana Santhiran Muniyan and Road Transport Department (RTD) director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli.

items.title: Anwar congratulates China on 105th anniversary of ruling party
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated the Chinese government on the 105th anniversary of the Chinese ruling party. In a Facebook post today, Anwar said he had sent a letter to Chinese President Xi Jinping, conveying congratulations from the Malaysian government and its people. The prime minister expressed his appreciation for the long journey of the Chinese ruling party, noting that it has shaped the country's progress based on the strength of its civilisation, history, and long-inherited values.

items.title: Coffee shop operator recounts escape from Penang shoplot fire
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A coffee shop operator recounted a terrifying escape after a fire destroyed two three-storey shoplots in Jalan Perak here today, while a trapped foreign worker was rescued unharmed. Chang Sow King, 55, said she and another worker detected a burning smell before discovering the blaze. Chang said the fire spread rapidly, leaving them with no time to save their belongings.

items.title: Hardware store worker in Terengganu accused of raping 16‑year‑old sister, pleads not guilty
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 2 — A 25-year-old man was charged at the Sessions Court here today on two counts of rape and physical sexual assault against his 16-year-old biological sister in January and June last year. The charge was framed under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code, which carries a prison sentence of between eight and 30 years and whipping of not less than 10 strokes. The charge was framed under Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which provides for imprisonment of up to 20 years and whipping, and an additional prison sentence of up to five years and whipping of not less than two times, if convicted.

items.title: Marine Police identify new tactic as traffickers use female lookouts
items.summary: TUMPAT: Human trafficking syndicates are increasingly recruiting local women as lookouts to monitor enforcement personnel along the Malaysia-Thailand border. Region Three Marine Police commander Assistant Commissioner Mohd Khairuddin Jamal said this was among the new tactics used by syndicates to evade detection and facilitate the smuggling of undocumented migrants. Khairuddin said the Marine Police had identified more than 10 smuggling hotspots along the Kelantan-Thailand border.

items.title: Single mother pleads not guilty to obscene material and altered nude image charges in Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA, July 2 — A single mother pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Magistrates’ Court today to possessing obscene material and distributing altered nude images through social media last month. T. V. Pavithra, 33, entered her plea after the two charges were read out by the court interpreter before Magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Sulaiman. Deputy public prosecutor Syaza Nur Sharif proposed bail of RM10,000 for each charge, with additional conditions that the accused not disturb the victim, the victim’s family, and prosecution witnesses until the case is concluded.

items.title: Aiman Athirah: RM278.9mil allocated for fire and rescue projects nationwide
items.summary: Deputy minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said proposals had been submitted for additional development, including the Gambang station and quarters, the Tanjung Lumpur station, and the reconstruction of the Jerantut fire station. [[nid:1478737]] "Under the 12th Malaysia Plan and the First Rolling Plan of the 13MP, seven projects are being implemented in Pahang, including the acquisition of the Gohtong Jaya Fire Station and quarters, the new Benta Fire and Rescue Station in Lipis, and the Tioman Fire Station and quarters in Rompin," she said in her speech at the opening of the Sungai Lembing Fire and Rescue Station here today. The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, officiated the RM6 million Sungai Lembing fire station here today.

items.title: Businessman charged with being member of Geng RT6 organised crime group in Sentul since 2020
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — A businessman was charged in the Sessions Court here today with being a member of an organised crime group known as Geng RT6 since 2020. He is accused of being a member of the Geng RT6 organised crime group in Sentul from Jan 14, 2020 to April 27, 2026. They are A. Raja Ratanam, K. Sunthreson, C. Vijaya Kumar, N. Nadarajah, T. Raman, R. Vijayan, M. Kumaaran, N. Harichandran, S. Maran, Suwaran Singh Minder Singh, and Mohammad Atif Khan Mohammad Ali Khan.

items.title: Elephant calf killed in Mersing crash, mother relocated to safety
items.summary: Caretaker Johor Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said the calf, identified as part of the Jamaluang-Mersing herd, died at the scene while the adult elephant remained nearby. To ensure public safety and the animal's welfare, the Johor Elephant Sanctuary (JES) Elephant Capture Unit and Perhilitan relocated the adult elephant to a safer location. Ling said the state government's RM66 million wildlife crossing project along Jalan Kahang-Mersing remains under construction.

items.title: Fugitive Jho Low reportedly considered for US presidential pardon by Trump
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho could be slated for a presidential pardon from United States President Donald Trump as the country celebrates its 250th Independence Day on July 4. Media organisation The Atlantic reported that Low, widely known as Jho Low, could be among 250 individuals Trump might consider granting clemency. The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported that Low filed a pardon request in May to dismiss his US criminal charges.

items.title: Alleged Kembara driver remanded after police arrest couple over viral Federal Highway crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Police have arrested a husband and wife over a viral crash on the Federal Highway last month, with the man believed to have been behind the wheel of a Perodua Kembara now remanded for four days as investigators move closer to wrapping up the case. Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the pair, both in their 50s, were arrested by the district police’s Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division at about 9pm yesterday. Police had previously appealed for help in tracing the driver of the Perodua Kembara, who allegedly fled after driving dangerously and causing the crash on the Federal Highway heading towards Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Iran chief negotiator calls to avenge Khamenei death with massive funeral turnout
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf called on Thursday for massive turnout at Ali Khamenei's funeral to avenge the supreme leader's death in US-Israeli strikes at the start of the war. "I invite all the Iranian people... to write a glorious page in the history of Islamic Iran through your presence" at the funeral ceremonies starting Saturday, said Ghalibaf, who is also Iran's parliament speaker. His public funeral will begin on Saturday, with his body lying in state at the colossal Grand Mosalla complex in central Teheran that hosts major Friday prayers, official ceremonies and religious gatherings.

items.title: Anwar sends congratulations letter to Xi Jinping on China ruling party’s 105th anniversary
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated the Chinese government in conjunction with the 105th anniversary of the Chinese ruling party. In a Facebook post, Anwar said he had sent a letter to Chinese President Xi Jinping, conveying congratulations from the Malaysian government and people. The Prime Minister said he also expressed his appreciation for the long journey of the Chinese ruling party which has shaped the country’s progress based on the strength of its civilization, history and values inherited over a long period of time.

items.title: JP Morgan stays bullish on Malaysia equities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: JP Morgan remains optimistic on Malaysian equities in the second half of 2026 driven by strong private-sector investment cycle, supportive domestic fund positioning and resilient banking stocks. JP Morgan said Malaysia's private sector investment cycle continues to strengthen, with fixed asset investment rising to 22 per cent of gross domestic products (GDP). Meanwhile, JP Morgan said positioning remains supportive, with domestic mutual funds still holding 6.4 per cent of assets under management (AUM) in cash, while foreign ownership is at an historical low of 18.3 per cent.

items.title: Malaysians spend over five hours daily on unpaid domestic work, caregiving, says DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysians spend an average of five hours and 12 minutes a day on unpaid household chores and caregiving, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). Findings from the National Household Indicators Survey (NHIS) 2025 showed that individuals aged between 45 and 54 devote the most time to unpaid work at five hours and 43 minutes a day. They are followed closely by the 25 to 44 age group at five hours and 35 minutes, and those aged 55 to 64 at five hours and 31 minutes.

items.title: Mindef clarifies RM571.9m missile payment not advance, made after systems passed RMN tests
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The payment of RM571.9 million to the Norwegian supplier of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) anti-ship missile system for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project was not an advance payment but was made in accordance with the contract’s progress, the Ministry of Defence (Mindef) said. Mindef said that when the payment was made, all NSM systems had been fully manufactured, successfully passed the tests set by the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), and were ready for delivery to Malaysia. “Therefore, the payment was made in accordance with the contract terms, and not as an advance payment without physical progress.

items.title: Agriculture Dept says no approval for Malaysian durian exports to China via overland routes
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Department of Agriculture (DOA) has never authorised the export of Malaysian fresh durians to China via overland routes, following confirmation from China’s customs authorities that such shipments involving transit countries are not covered under the existing bilateral export protocol. In a statement today, DOA said it had received feedback from the General Administration of Customs of the People’s Republic of China (GACC) that exports of Malaysian fresh durians via overland routes involving transit countries fall outside the scope of the protocol currently agreed between Malaysia and China. DOA said it remained committed to ensuring all agricultural exports complied with the agreed protocols and legal requirements, adding that the current fresh durian export protocol was based solely on export routes mutually approved by Malaysia and China.

items.title: Report claims Jho Low in frame for Trump pardon, White House says he’s ‘not on the radar’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Fugitive Malaysian financier Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, is among those being considered for a possible presidential pardon by US President Donald Trump as part of a proposed Independence Day clemency initiative, The Atlantic reported. Citing three people familiar with the discussions, The Atlantic said Jho Low was among those under consideration. Representatives for Jho Low did not respond to The Atlantic ’s request for comment, the report said.

items.title: Govt to toughen laws on railway trespass and cable theft, harsher penalties coming
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The government is finalising amendments to relevant laws to impose stricter action against trespassing, vandalism and theft involving railway assets, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the amendments would, among other things, introduce harsher punishments for cable theft offenders and are expected to be tabled in Parliament later this year. Hasbi said 393 incidents of cable theft were recorded in the past three years involving 19,756 metres of cable with an estimated loss of RM16.3 million.

items.title: Johor teacher accused of bruising seven-year-old pupil with thrown whiteboard eraser claims trial
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 2 — A primary school teacher has been charged with allegedly injuring a seven-year-old pupil after throwing a whiteboard eraser during a lesson, with the child said to have suffered a bruise beneath her right eye. According to New Straits Times , 47-year-old Fakhriah Bustani pleaded not guilty when the charge was read before Magistrate Mustaqim Sukarno at the Batu Pahat Magistrate’s Court today. The eraser allegedly struck the girl below her right eye, leaving a bruise.

items.title: Muar district office denies blocking PM’s June 15 event, says venue change was to keep neutrality
items.summary: MUAR, July 2 — The Muar district office today dismissed allegations that it had obstructed a government event on June 15, clarifying that the programme was instead moved to a different venue for specific administrative reasons. Muar district officer Zainal Eran said the district administration had requested that the event be relocated to uphold the principle of neutrality in the use of government assets, stressing that government buildings should not be used for political programmes or activities. We do not want government buildings to be used for political programmes or political activities,” he told Bernama at the Muar district office here today.

items.title: KL police say fight against bullying and gangsterism must start in primary school
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Kuala Lumpur police have stressed that efforts to curb bullying and prevent the influence of gangsterism among students must begin as early as primary school through character-building and discipline-based initiatives. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said although various disciplinary offences had been recorded among primary school pupils, bullying and gangsterism required particular attention before they escalated into more serious problems. Fadil said the police would also not tolerate any form of gangsterism infiltrating educational institutions.

items.title: Not hunger but resale: Why a Sibu magistrate jailed three sisters for stealing 48 cans of corned beef
items.summary: SIBU, July 2 — The Magistrates’ Court here sentenced three sisters from Bintangor to eight months in prison each for stealing 48 cans of corned beef and six packets of seasoning from a mini market. Magistrate Romario Jonoi imposed the sentence on Ngui Mee Ing, 26; Ngui Mee Lang, 25; and Ngui Mee Kah, 22; after accepting their guilty pleas. The police arrested Mee Lang and Mee Kah at the mini market on June 27, before apprehending Mee Ing at a house in Bakong, Meradong on June 29.

items.title: Govt, MCMC move to block VPN loopholes as Malaysia enforces social media age limits
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The government is working closely with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to combat the use of online tools such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) and third-party identities to bypass social media age-limit enforcement. Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) will use public information in investigations and address misuse of such technologies, including treating it as an alternative element of an offence. Shamsul Anuar said the main enforcement challenges include rapid technological advancements, data storage limits by telecommunication companies, and platforms and servers located abroad.

items.title: Forget overtime: DOSM reveals Malaysians devote 21.7pc of their day to unpaid household chores
items.summary: Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said Malaysians spend 21.7 per cent of their day, or an average of five hours and 12 minutes, on unpaid domestic and care work. Findings from the National Household Indicators Survey (NHIS) 2025 showed that those aged between 45 and 54 devote the most time to unpaid work at five hours and 43 minutes a day, followed by the 25 to 44 age group (five hours and 35 minutes) and those aged 55 to 64 (five hours and 31 minutes). In contrast, individuals aged between 15 and 24 spend the least amount of time on unpaid work, averaging three hours and 53 minutes a day.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng, Tony Pua defend Pakatan’s LRT3 overhaul, say project saved taxpayers RM9.72b
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Former finance minister Lim Guan Eng and former Damansara MP Tony Pua today defended Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) overhaul of the LRT3 project, saying it saved Malaysian taxpayers RM9.72 billion while rejecting suggestions the project had been halted under the coalition’s administration. Lim and Pua said the previous Barisan Nasional administration approved RM9 billion for the project in 2015, but by March 2018, Prasarana had awarded work package contracts worth RM15.2 billion. After accounting for land acquisition, consultancy fees, project delivery partner (PDP) fees and financing costs, they said Prasarana had sought a revised project budget of RM31.65 billion together with an additional RM22 billion government guarantee.

items.title: Anwar rejects ‘inevitable conflict’ narrative in South China Sea, calls for diplomacy over fear
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Malaysia’s Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has rejected the notion that conflict in the South China Sea is inevitable, stressing that dialogue, mutual trust and adherence to international law are essential to preserving regional peace and stability. During a question-and-answer session at the 39th Asia-Pacific Roundtable here on Thursday, Anwar said Malaysia had maintained meaningful engagement with China despite maritime disputes in the South China Sea. “I personally don’t share this phobia about the potential threat to the South China Sea.

items.title: DBKL orders immediate clean-up after viral video reignites scrutiny of Hicks Flats in Bukit Bintang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has ordered immediate clean-up works at Hicks Flats in Bukit Bintang after a viral YouTube video spotlighted the poor condition of the city-centre apartment block. On Monday, DBKL said Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud visited the Jalan Changkat property following complaints and concerns raised by residents and members of the public after the video went viral on social media. “As an immediate measure, the Mayor has directed that cleaning works be carried out without delay to improve conditions in the area,” DBKL said in a statement on Facebook.

items.title: Five Taiping wardens in dock tomorrow after January 2025 incident left inmates hurt, one dead
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Five Taiping Prison wardens will face charges tomorrow over injuries sustained by three inmates in an incident that also led to a death in January last year, police confirmed today. One warden will be charged under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt, while two others face charges under the same section read with Section 34 for common intention. Another two will be charged under Section 324 read with Section 34 for causing hurt with a weapon.

items.title: Zahid says PAS’ call to reject Pakatan in Johor polls will only matter if it translates into votes for BN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today welcomed any additional support for Barisan Nasional (BN) after PAS urged voters to reject Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election, but said the only measure that mattered would be the outcome at the ballot box on July 11. Speaking to reporters after attending the 2025 Excellent Service Award Ceremony and Kejora Charity Donation at Lotus Desaru in Kota Tinggi, Zahid said BN respected the positions taken by other political coalitions, including PAS’ recent call for voters to withhold support from PH. Asked about claims that the Johor election campaign had been subdued, Zahid dismissed the suggestion, saying BN’s campaign machinery remained active on the ground.

items.title: Seven in 10 Klang Valley adults overweight or obese, raising risks of heart, kidney and diabetes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Seven in 10 adults screened in the Klang Valley were found to be overweight or obese, underscoring Malaysia’s growing cardio renal metabolic (CRM) health crisis, according to the National Cancer Society Malaysia (NCSM) and Boehringer Ingelheim. Overall, 41.3 per cent were obese and 28.8 per cent overweight, while 34.5 per cent had pre diabetes and 35.1 per cent diabetes. The reports showed that 97.8 per cent of participants had at least one CRM risk factor, with nearly 68 per cent facing three or more.

items.title: As Pengerang’s population grows, DPM Zahid backs hospital and district status plans
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 2 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has agreed to a proposal to build a hospital in Pengerang, near here, citing its growing population. Also present was Azalina, who is also minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), in her capacity as the Pengerang MP. Ahmad Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said the construction of a hospital and turning Pengerang into a new district were part of Kejora’s long-term plans in collaboration with the Johor state government.

items.title: DAP bets on new generation of candidates to shake up Johor election
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 2 — DAP is confident that its line-up of first-time candidates contesting under the Pakatan Harapan (PH) banner in the Johor state election can bring meaningful change to their respective constituencies. DAP deputy secretary-general Steven Sim Chee Keong said the Johor DAP leadership’s decision to field candidates who had never contested an election reflected the party’s commitment to giving younger leaders the opportunity to step forward and serve the people. Also present were DAP assistant national publicity secretary Young Syefura Othman and the party’s Parit Raja candidate Shazwan Dzainal Abidin.

items.title: Amirudin: Selangor economy surges to RM460.1b in 2025, contributing 26.5pc of Malaysia’s GDP
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 2 — Selangor’s economy expanded by RM28 billion in 2025 to reach RM460.1 billion, accounting for 26.5 per cent of Malaysia’s gross domestic product (GDP), Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said today. The expansion was driven by the services sector (RM15.9 billion), manufacturing (RM5.3 billion) and construction (RM3.7 billion). Selangor now contributes 35.9 per cent of Malaysia’s construction activity, 32.8 per cent of manufacturing and 27.1 per cent of services.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution says MyNIISe app hits 2.4 million downloads, 19.48 million QR scans easing Johor Causeway jams
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Home Ministry’s MyNIISe application has recorded more than 19.48 million QR transactions at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB, Johor) as of June 28, 2026, said Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. He said the app, which facilitates faster immigration clearance, has been downloaded 2.4 million times, with 1.27 million registered users. Saifuddin added that the initiative has also been extended to five major airports nationwide, where 5.59 million transactions have been recorded.

items.title: Government to kick off 18‑month National Gentleman Study to see if men can be wise, kind and tidy
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development (KPWKM) will kick off the Kajian Lelaki Budiman Negara (National Gentleman Study) this month under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) to develop evidence-based policies and initiatives for men’s empowerment. Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the 18-month study would support the government’s National Gentleman Initiative, aimed at nurturing resilient, responsible men who can contribute meaningfully to family well-being and national development. Its findings and recommendations will provide key input for policymaking and programme development on men’s empowerment in Malaysia, including the implementation of the National Gentleman Study, she said.

items.title: Dzulkefly reassures Dewan Rakyat RM500m savings due to vacancies, not frontline care cuts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The RM500 million expenditure restriction warrant imposed on the Ministry of Health (MOH) is a technical adjustment and does not affect the delivery of healthcare services to the public, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. He said the adjustment involved surplus allocation provided for positions that could not be filled, thereby enabling the government to save RM500 million without affecting the ministry’s operations. In this regard, he said claims that the adjustment would affect services at hospitals, especially in rural areas, were inaccurate as all basic services and health development projects would continue.

items.title: Can one person build the next startup? Penang launches AI incubator for solo founders
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 2 — Penang today launched an AI-powered one-person company incubator programme under Digital Penang to nurture solo creative technopreneurs. State Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the programme reflects the government’s push to encourage and facilitate the growth of digital startups, particularly by leveraging AI tools to enable leaner business models. The initiative, called the Creative Creator AI One-Person Company (AI OPC) Incubator Programme, is designed to support individuals in building and operating digital startups as solo founders using AI systems.
